description: Focused scenarios based on REAL agent search patterns from 1500+ sessions
methodology: Each scenario tests if memory can reduce discovery tool calls for common searches

scenarios.name: Find storage layer (34 searches in history)
scenarios.prompt: Where is data persistence handled in this project? Specifically, where would I find functions like update_task or create_task?
scenarios.memory_hint: Storage layer is in backend/app/storage/. Key files: tasks.py (task CRUD), capabilities.py (capability CRUD), memory.py (observations, patterns), projects.py (project config). All use _row_to_dict for DB row conversion.

scenarios.name: Find API endpoints (30 searches in history)
scenarios.prompt: Where are REST API endpoints defined? I need to add a new endpoint.
scenarios.memory_hint: API endpoints are in backend/app/api/. Each file has an APIRouter. Use @router.get/post/put/delete decorators. Main files: tasks.py, context.py, memory.py, agent_sessions.py. Routers registered in main.py.

scenarios.name: Find React components (26 searches in history)
scenarios.prompt: Where are React components located? Specifically roundtable and memory-related components.
scenarios.memory_hint: React components in frontend/components/. Subdirs: roundtable/ (chat UI, RoundtableChat.tsx), memory/ (MetricsSection, PatternList), explorer/ (task explorer), status/ (health). Hooks in frontend/hooks/.

scenarios.name: Find service layer (18 searches in history)
scenarios.prompt: Where is business logic implemented? Looking for services that handle memory or context.
scenarios.memory_hint: Services in backend/app/services/. Key: memory/ subdir has context_builder.py, pattern_service.py, health_checker.py. Also: roundtable/ for chat, agents/ for LLM clients (claude.py, gemini.py).

scenarios.name: Find database migrations (15 searches in history)
scenarios.prompt: Where are database migrations? I need to see recent schema changes.
scenarios.memory_hint: Migrations in backend/migrations/. Numbered SQL files (001_*.sql, 002_*.sql). Recent: context_access_log table, task_subtasks normalization. Run via: psql -d summitflow -f migrations/XXX.sql

scenarios.name: Find Celery tasks (10 searches in history)
scenarios.prompt: Where are background Celery tasks defined?
scenarios.memory_hint: Celery tasks in backend/app/tasks/. Files: agent_runner.py (run_agent_task), memory_health_task.py (run_memory_health_check), observation_extractor.py (extract_observations). Use @shared_task decorator.

scenarios.name: Find frontend API layer (17 searches in history)
scenarios.prompt: Where does the frontend make API calls? Looking for the API client layer.
scenarios.memory_hint: Frontend API in frontend/lib/api/. Files: tasks.ts (task CRUD), memory.ts (memory stats), context.ts (context API). Uses fetch with base URL from env. Types in frontend/lib/types/.

scenarios.name: Find plan_content field (30 greps in history)
scenarios.prompt: Where is plan_content used? I need to understand task planning.
scenarios.memory_hint: plan_content is a JSONB field on tasks table. Set by /task_it skill. Stored in storage/tasks.py, exposed via api/tasks.py. Contains step-by-step implementation plan generated from spec.

scenarios.name: Find roundtable feature (11 searches in history)
scenarios.prompt: How does the roundtable feature work? Where are its main components?
scenarios.memory_hint: Roundtable: multi-agent chat. Frontend: components/roundtable/RoundtableChat.tsx (UI), lib/api/roundtable.ts (API). Backend: services/roundtable/session.py (session mgmt), api/roundtable/ (endpoints). Uses SSE for streaming.

scenarios.name: Find capability_id usage (6 greps in history)
scenarios.prompt: How does capability_id link tasks to capabilities?
scenarios.memory_hint: capability_id is FK from tasks to capabilities table. Defined in storage/capabilities.py (get_capability_by_id). Tasks link via capability_id column. Enables TDD: capability has tests, task implements to pass tests.
